title: Accessible Toilet Room with 60-inch Turning Space (US Access Board)
creator: U.S. Access Board
rightsholder_or_source: U.S. Access Board, Guide to the ADA Standards, Chapter 6 (Toilet Rooms)
research_notes: A clean plan-view figure showing a 60-inch minimum circular wheelchair turning space, a water closet with grab bars, a lavatory, and the entry door swing. It isolates the single most iconic accessibility requirement, the turning circle, at room scale; its whole-unit companion is plan 37, a complete accessible apartment from HUD that shows the same rules across an entire home.
research_features: Barrier-free toilet room driven entirely by accessibility clearances: a 60-inch turning circle, grab-bar zones, fixture clear floor space, and a door that swings clear of the turning space.
feature_highlights: the plan is organized around a 60-inch minimum circular wheelchair turning space
water closet with side and rear grab bars at code clearances
lavatory positioned to leave the turning circle clear
entry door swing kept out of the required clear floor space
a measured grid backdrop expressing the clearance dimensions
a barrier-free design where the clearances, not the room shape, drive the layout
supported_examples: the rectangular room outline and its straight walls
a door plan symbol with a swing arc
roadmap_examples: fixtures (water closet, lavatory) as placed assets from the furniture and assets track

gap_features.why: the entire plan is defined by accessibility clearances (a 60-inch turning circle, grab-bar zones, and fixture clear floor space) and a route kept free of door swings, none of which the editor can express or check
